pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the expansion of the universe and the heat death thing... and it just hit me how much of a joke everything is. like, we spend our whole lives struggling and feeling things just for it to all be erased eventually. doesn't it just feel like we're just performing this pointless ritual for no reason?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back again and it's just... nothing. another year of work down the drain. my funding is basically gone and i feel like i'm just a ghost moving through the halls. i’ve spent my whole life being this "promising student" and now there's nothing left. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to exist in this loop. everything feels so heavy and i just want the weight to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the waning embers of my own spirit. How cruel it is, that after all the vows whispered under the willow trees, I am left with nothing but this hollow ache in my chest. The world has grown cold, and the shadows seem to reach for me, more welcoming than the light ever was. I feel as though I am already a ghost, wandering a life that no longer holds a place for me. Is there truly any mercy left in this world, or is the only peace to be found in the silent, eternal dark?
